page: Microsoft Azure D32plds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server provides 32 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading resource sharing. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is supported by a 1229 GB local disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 57238.05 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12092.0. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1033.0 and a weak text processing score of 1384.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 143.96 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 69.76 GB/sec for large-block write operations. While multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches an average score of 61.11 GB/sec, single-threaded bzip3 decompression drops to a poor rating of 6.99 GB/sec.

The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this server as a compute-focused option with lower memory density. This design offers qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, leveraging the power-efficient ARM architecture. However, the lack of advanced vector and matrix extensions, such as AVX or AMX, limits its suitability for heavy mathematical acceleration. Large-scale LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with the 70B model achieving poor speeds of 3.6 tokens/sec for prompt processing. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, and database operations.
description: Microsoft Azure D32plds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, representing a 1:2 core-to-memory ratio, and includes a 1229 GB local disk. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the server delivers average multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 12092.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 57238.05. However, single-core metrics are weaker, and the lack of AVX or AMX extensions restricts high-performance vector math. This server offers qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads that require dedicated physical cores but lower memory overhead. It is suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations, while being poorly suited for large language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32plds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and a 1229 GB local disk, delivering balanced multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32plds_v5 features 32 dedicated ARM64 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring thirty-two dedicated cores and local storage for efficient multi-threaded processing and web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor with 32 dedicated physical cores.
Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory and a 1229 GB local disk.
Delivers average multi-core performance but exhibits weaker single-core benchmark results.
Lacks AVX, AMX, and hyperthreading capabilities, limiting vector-heavy compute tasks.
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded database operations.
